String.name: String
String.description: Alpha-numeric free format strings, can include any character or punctuation except the delimiter. All String fields are case-sensitive (i.e. morstatt != Morstatt).

char.name: char
char.description: Single character value, can include any alphanumeric character or punctuation except the delimiter. All char fields are case sensitive (i.e. m != M).

Price.name: Price
Price.description: float field representing a price. Note the number of decimal places may vary. For certain asset classes prices may be negative values. For example, prices for options strategies can be negative under certain market conditions. Refer to Volume 7: FIX Usage by Product for asset classes that support negative price values.

SeqNum.name: SeqNum
SeqNum.description: int field representing a message sequence number. Value must be positive.

Length.name: Length
Length.description: int field representing the length in bytes. Value must be positive.

Amt.name: Amt
Amt.description: float field typically representing a Price times a Qty

Qty.name: Qty
Qty.description: float field capable of storing either a whole number (no decimal places) of "shares" (securities denominated in whole units) or a decimal value containing decimal places for non-share quantity asset classes (securities denominated in fractional units).

Currency.name: Currency
Currency.description: string field representing a currency type using ISO 4217 Currency code (3 character) values (see Appendix 6-A).

MultipleValueString.name: MultipleValueString
MultipleValueString.description: string field containing one or more space delimited single character values.

Exchange.name: Exchange
Exchange.description: string field representing a market or exchange using ISO 10383 Market Identifier Code (MIC) values (see Appendix 6-C).

NumInGroup.name: NumInGroup
NumInGroup.description: int field representing the number of entries in a repeating group. Value must be positive.

UTCTimestamp.name: UTCTimestamp
UTCTimestamp.description: string field representing Time/date combination represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in either YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS (whole seconds) or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.sss (milliseconds) format, colons, dash, and period required. Valid values: * YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-60 (60 only if UTC leap second) (without milliseconds). * YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-60 (60 only if UTC leap second), sss=000-999 (indicating milliseconds). Leap Seconds: Note that UTC includes corrections for leap seconds, which are inserted to account for slowing of the rotation of the earth. Leap second insertion is declared by the International Earth Rotation Service (IERS) and has, since 1972, only occurred on the night of Dec. 31 or Jun 30. The IERS considers March 31 and September 30 as secondary dates for leap second insertion, but has never utilized these dates. During a leap second insertion, a UTCTimestamp field may read "1998-12-31T23:59:59", "1998-12-31T23:59:60", "1999-01-01T00:00:00". (see http://tycho.usno.navy.mil/leapsec.html)

Boolean.name: Boolean
Boolean.description: char field containing one of two values: 'Y' = True/Yes 'N' = False/No

LocalMktDate.name: LocalMktDate
LocalMktDate.description: string field representing a Date of Local Market (as opposed to UTC) in YYYY-MM-DD format per the ISO 8601 standard. This is the "normal" date field used by the FIX Protocol. Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31.

int.name: int
int.description: Sequence of digits without commas or decimals and optional sign character (ASCII characters "-" and "0" - "9" ). The sign character utilizes one byte (i.e. positive int is "99999" while negative int is "-99999"). Note that int values may contain leading zeros (e.g. "00023" = "23"). Examples: 723 in field 21 would be mapped int as |21=723|. -723 in field 12 would be mapped int as |12=-723| The following data types are based on int.

data.name: data
data.description: Raw data with no format or content restrictions. Data fields are always immediately preceded by a length field. The length field should specify the number of bytes of the value of the data field (up to but not including the terminating SOH). Caution: the value of one of these fields may contain the delimiter (SOH) character. Note that the value specified for this field should be followed by the delimiter (SOH) character as all fields are terminated with an "SOH".

float.name: float
float.description: Sequence of digits with optional decimal point and sign character (ASCII characters "-", "0" - "9" and "."); the absence of the decimal point within the string will be interpreted as the float representation of an integer value. All float fields must accommodate up to fifteen significant digits. The number of decimal places used should be a factor of business/market needs and mutual agreement between counterparties. Note that float values may contain leading zeros (e.g. "00023.23" = "23.23") and may contain or omit trailing zeros after the decimal point (e.g. "23.0" = "23.0000" = "23" = "23."). Note that fields which are derived from float may contain negative values unless explicitly specified otherwise.

Percentage.name: Percentage
Percentage.description: float field representing a percentage (e.g. 0.05 represents 5% and 0.9525 represents 95.25%). Note the number of decimal places may vary.

PriceOffset.name: PriceOffset
PriceOffset.description: float field representing a price offset, which can be mathematically added to a "Price". Note the number of decimal places may vary and some fields such as LastForwardPoints may be negative.

MonthYear.name: MonthYear
MonthYear.description: string field representing month of a year. An optional day of the month can be appended or an optional week code. Valid formats: YYYYMM YYYYMMDD YYYYMMWW Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999; MM = 01-12; DD = 01-31; WW = w1, w2, w3, w4, w5.

UTCDateOnly.name: UTCDateOnly
UTCDateOnly.description: string field representing Date represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in YYYY-MM-DD format specified by ISO 8601. This special-purpose field is paired with UTCTimeOnly to form a proper UTCTimestamp for bandwidth-sensitive messages. Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31.

UTCTimeOnly.name: UTCTimeOnly
UTCTimeOnly.description: string field representing Time-only represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in either HH:MM:SS (whole seconds) or HH:MM:SS.sss (milliseconds) format, colons, and period required. This special-purpose field is paired with UTCDateOnly to form a proper UTCTimestamp for bandwidth-sensitive messages. Valid values: HH = 00-23, MM = 00-60 (60 only if UTC leap second), SS = 00-59. (without milliseconds) HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-60 (60 only if UTC leap second), sss=000-999 (indicating milliseconds).

Country.name: Country
Country.description: string field representing a country using ISO 3166 Country code (2 character) values (see Appendix 6-B).

1.name: Account
1.description: Account mnemonic as agreed between buy and sell sides, e.g. broker and institution or investor/intermediary and fund manager.

2.name: AdvId
2.description: Unique identifier of advertisement message. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

3.name: AdvRefID
3.description: Reference identifier used with CANCEL and REPLACE transaction types. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

6.name: AvgPx
6.description: Calculated average price of all fills on this order. For Fixed Income trades AvgPx is always expressed as percent-of-par, regardless of the PriceType (423) of LastPx (3). I.e., AvgPx will contain an average of percent-of-par values (see LastParPx (669)) for issues traded in Yield, Spread or Discount.

7.name: BeginSeqNo
7.description: Message sequence number of first message in range to be resent

8.name: BeginString
8.description: Identifies beginning of new message and protocol version. ALWAYS FIRST FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted)

9.name: BodyLength
9.description: Message length, in bytes, forward to the CheckSum field. ALWAYS SECOND FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted)

10.name: CheckSum
10.description: Three byte, simple checksum (see Volume 2: "Checksum Calculation" for description). ALWAYS LAST FIELD IN MESSAGE; i.e. serves, with the trailing <SOH>, as the end-of-message delimiter. Always defined as three characters. (Always unencrypted)

11.name: ClOrdID
11.description: Unique identifier for Order as assigned by the buy-side (institution, broker, intermediary etc.) (identified by SenderCompID(49) or OnBehalfOfCompID(115) as appropriate). Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms, particularly those which electronically submit multi-day orders, trade globally or throughout market close periods, should ensure uniqueness across days, for example by embedding a date within the ClOrdID(11) field.

12.name: Commission
12.description: Commission. Note if CommType (3) is percentage, Commission of 5% should be represented as .05.

enumValues.name: PER_UNIT
enumValues.description: per unit (implying shares, par, currency, etc)
(for CIV buy orders) percentage waived – cash discount
(for CIV buy orders) percentage waived – enhanced units

enumValues.name: POINTS_PER_BOND_OR_CONTRACT_SUPPLY_CONTRACTMULTIPLIER
enumValues.description: points per bond or contract [Supply ContractMultiplier (231) in the <Instrument> component block if the object security is denominated in a size other than the industry default - 1000 par for bonds.]

14.name: CumQty
14.description: Total quantity (e.g. number of shares) filled. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

15.name: Currency
15.description: Identifies currency used for price. Absence of this field is interpreted as the default for the security. It is recommended that systems provide the currency value whenever possible. See "Appendix 6-A: Valid Currency Codes" for information on obtaining valid values.

16.name: EndSeqNo
16.description: Message sequence number of last message in range to be resent. If request is for a single message BeginSeqNo (7) = EndSeqNo. If request is for all messages subsequent to a particular message, EndSeqNo = "0" (representing infinity).

17.name: ExecID
17.description: Unique identifier of execution message as assigned by sell-side (broker, exchange, ECN) (will be 0 (zero) for ExecType (50) =I (Order Status)). Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day or the life of a multi-day order. Firms which accept multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the ExecID field to assure uniqueness across days. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

18.name: ExecInst

enumValues.name: PERCENT_OF_VOLUME
enumValues.description: Percent of volume "(indicates that the sender does not want to be all the volume on the floor vs. a specific percentage)"

enumValues.name: REINSTATE_ON_SYSTEM_FAILURE
enumValues.description: Reinstate on System Failure (mutually exclusive with Q)

enumValues.name: REINSTATE_ON_TRADING_HALT
enumValues.description: Reinstate on Trading Halt (mutually exclusive with K)

enumValues.name: CANCEL_ON_TRADING_HALT
enumValues.description: Cancel on Trading Halt (mutually exclusive with L)

enumValues.name: CANCEL_ON_SYSTEM_FAILURE
enumValues.description: Cancel on System Failure (mutually exclusive with H)

enumValues.name: PRIMARY_PEG
enumValues.description: Primary peg (primary market - buy at bid/sell at offer)

18.description: Instructions for order handling on exchange trading floor. If more than one instruction is applicable to an order, this field can contain multiple instructions separated by space.

19.name: ExecRefID
19.description: Reference identifier used with Trade Cancel and Trade Correct execution types. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

21.name: HandlInst

enumValues.name: AUTOMATED_EXECUTION_ORDER_PRIVATE_NO_BROKER_INTERVENTION
enumValues.description: Automated execution order, private, no Broker intervention

enumValues.name: AUTOMATED_EXECUTION_ORDER_PUBLIC_BROKER_INTERVENTION_OK
enumValues.description: Automated execution order, public, Broker intervention OK

21.description: Instructions for order handling on Broker trading floor

22.name: SecurityIDSource

enumValues.name: CONSOLIDATED_TAPE_ASSOCIATION
enumValues.description: Consolidated Tape Association (CTA) Symbol (SIAC CTS/CQS line format)

22.description: Identifies class or source of the SecurityID (48) value. Required if SecurityID is specified.

23.name: IOIID
23.description: Unique identifier of IOI message. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

26.name: IOIRefID
26.description: Reference identifier used with CANCEL and REPLACE, transaction types. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

27.name: IOIQty
27.description: Quantity (e.g. number of shares) in numeric form or relative size.

30.name: LastMkt
30.description: Market of execution for last fill, or an indication of the market where an order was routed Valid values: See "Appendix 6-C"

32.name: LastQty
32.description: Quantity (e.g. shares) bought/sold on this (last) fill. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

33.name: NoLinesOfText
33.description: Identifies number of lines of text body

35.name: MsgType

enumValues.name: CROSS_ORDER_CANCEL_REPLACE_REQUEST
enumValues.description: Cross Order Cancel/Replace Request (a.k.a. Cross Order Modification Request)

enumValues.name: MULTILEG_ORDER_CANCEL_REPLACE
enumValues.description: Multileg Order Cancel/Replace (a.k.a. Multileg Order Modification Request)

enumValues.name: ALLOCATION_REPORT_ACK
enumValues.description: Allocation Report Ack (aka Allocation Claim Ack)

35.description: Defines message type ALWAYS THIRD FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted) Note: A "U" as the first character in the MsgType field (i.e. U, U2, etc) indicates that the message format is privately defined between the sender and receiver.

37.name: OrderID
37.description: Unique identifier for Order as assigned by sell-side (broker, exchange, ECN). Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms which accept multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the OrderID field to assure uniqueness across days.

38.name: OrderQty
38.description: Quantity ordered. This represents the number of shares for equities or par, face or nominal value for FI instruments. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

39.name: OrdStatus

enumValues.name: PENDING_CANCEL
enumValues.description: Pending Cancel (e.g. result of Order Cancel Request)

enumValues.name: PENDING_REPLACE
enumValues.description: Pending Replace (e.g. result of Order Cancel/Replace Request)

39.description: Identifies current status of order. *** SOME VALUES HAVE BEEN REPLACED - See "Replaced Features and Supported Approach" *** (see Volume : "Glossary" for value definitions)

40.name: OrdType

enumValues.name: FUNARI
enumValues.description: Funari (Limit Day Order with unexecuted portion handled as Market On Close. E.g. Japan)

enumValues.name: MARKET_WITH_LEFTOVER_AS_LIMIT
enumValues.description: Market with Leftover as Limit (market order then unexecuted quantity becomes limit order at last price)

enumValues.name: PREVIOUS_FUND_VALUATION_POINT
enumValues.description: Previous Fund Valuation Point (Historic pricing) (for CIV)

enumValues.name: NEXT_FUND_VALUATION_POINT
enumValues.description: Next Fund Valuation Point –(Forward pricing) (for CIV)

40.description: Order type *** SOME VALUES ARE NO LONGER USED - See "Deprecated (Phased-out) Features and Supported Approach" *** (see Volume : "Glossary" for value definitions)

41.name: OrigClOrdID
41.description: ClOrdID () of the previous order (NOT the initial order of the day) as assigned by the institution, used to identify the previous order in cancel and cancel/replace requests.

42.name: OrigTime
42.description: Time of message origination (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT"))

43.name: PossDupFlag
43.description: Indicates possible retransmission of message with this sequence number

44.name: Price
44.description: Price per unit of quantity (e.g. per share)

48.name: SecurityID
48.description: Security identifier value of SecurityIDSource (22) type (e.g. CUSIP, SEDOL, ISIN, etc). Requires SecurityIDSource.

49.name: SenderCompID
49.description: Assigned value used to identify firm sending message.

50.name: SenderSubID
50.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator (desk, trader, etc.)

52.name: SendingTime
52.description: Time of message transmission (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

53.name: Quantity
53.description: Overall/total quantity (e.g. number of shares) (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

enumValues.name: UNDISCLOSED
enumValues.description: Undisclosed (valid for IOI and List Order messages only)

enumValues.name: CROSS
enumValues.description: Cross (orders where counterparty is an exchange, valid for all messages except IOIs)

55.name: Symbol
55.description: Ticker symbol. Common, "human understood" representation of the security. SecurityID (48) value can be specified if no symbol exists (e.g. non-exchange traded Collective Investment Vehicles) Use "[N/A]" for products which do not have a symbol.

56.name: TargetCompID
56.description: Assigned value used to identify receiving firm.

57.name: TargetSubID
57.description: Assigned value used to identify specific individual or unit intended to receive message. "ADMIN" reserved for administrative messages not intended for a specific user.

58.name: Text
58.description: Free format text string (Note: this field does not have a specified maximum length)

59.name: TimeInForce
59.description: Specifies how long the order remains in effect. Absence of this field is interpreted as DAY.

60.name: TransactTime
60.description: Time of execution/order creation (expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

62.name: ValidUntilTime
62.description: Indicates expiration time of indication message (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

63.name: SettlType
63.description: Indicates order settlement period. If present, SettlDate (64) overrides this field. If both SettlType (63) and SettDate (64) are omitted, the default for SettlType (63) is 0 (Regular) Regular is defined as the default settlement period for the particular security on the exchange of execution. In Fixed Income the contents of this field may influence the instrument definition if the SecurityID (48) is ambiguous. In the US an active Treasury offering may be re-opened, and for a time one CUSIP will apply to both the current and "when-issued" securities. Supplying a value of "7" clarifies the instrument description; any other value or the absence of this field should cause the respondent to default to the active issue.

64.name: SettlDate
64.description: Specific date of trade settlement (SettlementDate) in YYYYMMDD format. If present, this field overrides SettlType (63). This field is required if the value of SettlType (63) is 6 (Future) or 8 (Sellers Option). This field must be omitted if the value of SettlType (63) is 7 (When and If Issued) (expressed in local time at place of settlement)

65.name: SymbolSfx
65.description: Additional information about the security (e.g. preferred, warrants, etc.). Note also see SecurityType(167). Valid values: As defined in the NYSE Stock and bond Symbol Directory and in the AMEX Fitch Directory Fixed Income use: WI = "When Issued" for a security to be reissued under an old CUSIP or ISIN CD = a EUCP with lump-sum interest rather than discount price

66.name: ListID
66.description: Unique identifier for list as assigned by institution, used to associate multiple individual orders. Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms which generate multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the ListID field to assure uniqueness across days.

67.name: ListSeqNo
67.description: Sequence of individual order within list (i.e. ListSeqNo of TotNoOrders (68), 2 of 25, 3 of 25, . . . )

68.name: TotNoOrders
68.description: Total number of list order entries across all messages. Should be the sum of all NoOrders (73) in each message that has repeating list order entries related to the same ListID (66). Used to support fragmentation. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was named "ListNoOrds")

69.name: ListExecInst
69.description: Free format text message containing list handling and execution instructions.

70.name: AllocID
70.description: Unique identifier for allocation message. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

71.name: AllocTransType
71.description: Identifies allocation transaction type *** SOME VALUES HAVE BEEN REPLACED - See "Replaced Features and Supported Approach" ***

72.name: RefAllocID
72.description: Reference identifier to be used with AllocTransType (7) =Replace or Cancel. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

73.name: NoOrders
73.description: Indicates number of orders to be combined for average pricing and allocation.

74.name: AvgPxPrecision
74.description: Indicates number of decimal places to be used for average pricing. Absence of this field indicates that default precision arranged by the broker/institution is to be used.

75.name: TradeDate
75.description: Indicates date of trade referenced in this message in YYYYMMDD format. Absence of this field indicates current day (expressed in local time at place of trade).

77.name: PositionEffect
77.description: Indicates whether the resulting position after a trade should be an opening position or closing position. Used for omnibus accounting - where accounts are held on a gross basis instead of being netted together.

80.name: AllocQty
80.description: Quantity to be allocated to specific sub-account (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

81.name: ProcessCode
81.description: Processing code for sub-account. Absence of this field in AllocAccount (79) / AllocPrice (366) /AllocQty (80) / ProcessCode instance indicates regular trade.

82.name: NoRpts
82.description: Total number of reports within series.

83.name: RptSeq
83.description: Sequence number of message within report series.

84.name: CxlQty
84.description: Total quantity canceled for this order. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

85.name: NoDlvyInst
85.description: Number of delivery instruction fields in repeating group. Note this field was removed in FIX 4.1 and reinstated in FIX 4.4.

93.name: SignatureLength
93.description: Number of bytes in signature field.

95.name: RawDataLength
95.description: Number of bytes in raw data field.

96.name: RawData
96.description: Unformatted raw data, can include bitmaps, word processor documents, etc.

97.name: PossResend
97.description: Indicates that message may contain information that has been sent under another sequence number.

enumValues.name: PGP_DES_MD5
enumValues.description: PGP/DES-MD5 (see app note on FIX website)

enumValues.name: PEM_DES_MD5
enumValues.description: PEM/DES-MD5 (see app note on FIX website)

99.name: StopPx
99.description: Price per unit of quantity (e.g. per share)

100.name: ExDestination
100.description: Execution destination as defined by institution when order is entered. Valid values: See "Appendix 6-C"

102.name: CxlRejReason

enumValues.name: ORDER_ALREADY_IN_PENDING_CANCEL_OR_PENDING_REPLACE_STATUS
enumValues.description: Order already in Pending Cancel or Pending Replace status

enumValues.name: UNABLE_TO_PROCESS_ORDER_MASS_CANCEL_REQUEST
enumValues.description: Unable to process Order Mass Cancel Request

enumValues.description: OrigOrdModTime (586) did not match last TransactTime (60) of order

enumValues.description: Duplicate of a verbally communicated order

103.description: Code to identify reason for order rejection.

enumValues.name: AT_THE_CLOSE
enumValues.description: At the close (around/not held to close)

enumValues.name: AT_THE_MARKET
enumValues.description: At the Market (previously called Current Quote)

106.name: Issuer
106.description: Name of security issuer (e.g. International Business Machines, GNMA). see also Volume 7: "PRODUCT: FIXED INCOME - Euro Issuer Values"

110.name: MinQty
110.description: Minimum quantity of an order to be executed. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

111.name: MaxFloor
111.description: Maximum quantity (e.g. number of shares) within an order to be shown on the exchange floor at any given time. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

112.name: TestReqID
112.description: Identifier included in Test Request message to be returned in resulting Heartbeat

enumValues.name: YES

enumValues.description: Indicates that party sending message will report trade

enumValues.description: Indicates the broker is responsible for locating the stock

enumValues.name: NO
enumValues.description: Indicates the broker is not required to locate

114.description: Indicates whether the broker is to locate the stock in conjunction with a short sell order.

115.name: OnBehalfOfCompID
115.description: Assigned value used to identify firm originating message if the message was delivered by a third party i.e. the third party firm identifier would be delivered in the SenderCompID field and the firm originating the message in this field.

116.name: OnBehalfOfSubID
116.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator (i.e. trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

118.name: NetMoney
118.description: Total amount due as the result of the transaction (e.g. for Buy order - principal + commission + fees) reported in currency of execution.

119.name: SettlCurrAmt

122.name: OrigSendingTime
122.description: Original time of message transmission (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") when transmitting orders as the result of a resend request.

123.name: GapFillFlag

enumValues.name: YES
enumValues.description: Gap Fill message, MsgSeqNum field valid

123.description: Indicates that the Sequence Reset message is replacing administrative or application messages which will not be resent.

124.name: NoExecs
124.description: No of execution repeating group entries to follow.

126.name: ExpireTime
126.description: Time/Date of order expiration (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") The meaning of expiration is specific to the context where the field is used. For orders, this is the expiration time of a Good Til Date TimeInForce. For Quotes - this is the expiration of the quote. Expiration time is provided across the quote message dialog to control the length of time of the overall quoting process. For collateral requests, this is the time by which collateral must be assigned. For collateral assignments, this is the time by which a response to the assignment is expected.

128.name: DeliverToCompID
128.description: Assigned value used to identify the firm targeted to receive the message if the message is delivered by a third party i.e. the third party firm identifier would be delivered in the TargetCompID (56) field and the ultimate receiver firm ID in this field.

129.name: DeliverToSubID

130.name: IOINaturalFlag

134.name: BidSize
134.description: Quantity of bid (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

135.name: OfferSize
135.description: Quantity of offer (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

136.name: NoMiscFees

141.name: ResetSeqNumFlag
141.description: Indicates that the both sides of the FIX session should reset sequence numbers.

142.name: SenderLocationID
142.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader)

143.name: TargetLocationID
143.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message destination’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader)

144.name: OnBehalfOfLocationID
144.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

145.name: DeliverToLocationID
145.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message recipient’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

146.name: NoRelatedSym
146.description: Specifies the number of repeating symbols specified.

147.name: Subject
147.description: The subject of an Email message

148.name: Headline
148.description: The headline of a News message

149.name: URLLink

158.description: The amount the buyer compensates the seller for the portion of the next coupon interest payment the seller has earned but will not receive from the issuer because the issuer will send the next coupon payment to the buyer. 200.description: Can be used with standardized derivatives vs. the MaturityDate (541) field. Month and Year of the maturity (used for standardized futures and options). Format: YYYYMM (i.e. 199903) YYYYMMDD (20030323) YYYYMMwN (200303w) for week A specific date or can be appended to the MaturityMonthYear. For instance, if multiple standard products exist that mature in the same Year and Month, but actually mature at a different time, a value can be appended, such as "w" or "w2" to indicate week as opposed to week 2 expiration. Likewise, the date (0-3) can be appended to indicate a specific expiration (maturity date).

"European") For Exchanges: DTB (Frankfurt), HKSE (Hong Kong), and SOFFEX (Zurich) 0-9 = single digit "version" number assigned by exchange following capital adjustments (0=current, 1=prior, 2=prior to 1, etc).

To be expressed as "count of basis points" (vs. an absolute value). E.g. High Grade Corporate Bonds may express price as basis points relative to benchmark (the BenchmarkCurveName(221) field). Note: Basis points can be negative. Swap Spread: Target spread for a swap.

Sample values: M = combination of a number between 1-12 and a "M" for month Y = combination of number between 1-100 and a "Y" for year} 10Y-OLD = see above, then add "-OLD" when appropriate INTERPOLATED = the point is mathematically derived 2/2031 5 3/8 = the point is stated via a combination of maturity month / year and coupon See Fixed Income-specific documentation at http://www.fixprotocol.org for additional values. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

In TIPS securities this is the Inflation index. Qty * Factor * Price = Gross Trade Amount For Derivatives: Contract Value Factor by which price must be adjusted to determine the true nominal value of one futures/options contract. (Qty * Price) * Factor = Nominal Value (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

(Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

When quantity is expressed in contracts, e.g. financing transactions and bond trade reporting, ContractMultiplier should contain the number of units in one contract and can be omitted if the multiplier is the default amount for the instrument, i.e. 1,000 par of bonds, 1,000,000 par for financing transactions.

See also the Product(460) and SecurityType(167) fields. It is recommended that CFICode(461) be used instead of SecurityType(167) for non-Fixed Income instruments. A subset of possible values applicable to FIX usage are identified in "Appendix 6-D CFICode Usage - ISO 10962 Classification of Financial Instruments (CFI code)"

468.description: Specifies which direction to round For CIV – indicates whether or not the quantity of shares/units is to be rounded and in which direction where CashOrdQty (52) or (for CIV only) OrderPercent (56) are specified on an order. The default is for rounding to be at the discretion of the executing broker or fund manager. e.g. for an order specifying CashOrdQty or OrderPercent if the calculated number of shares/units was 325.76 and RoundingModulus (469) was 0 – "round down" would give 320 units, "round up" would give 330 units and "round to nearest" would give 320 units.

469.name: RoundingModulus
469.description: For CIV - a float value indicating the value to which rounding is required. i.e. 0 means round to a multiple of 0 units/shares; 0.5 means round to a multiple of 0.5 units/shares. The default, if RoundingDirection (468) is specified without RoundingModulus, is to round to a whole unit/share.

537.name: QuoteType
537.description: Identifies the type of quote. An indicative quote is used to inform a counterparty of a market. An indicative quote does not result directly in a trade. A tradeable quote is submitted to a market and will result directly in a trade against other orders and quotes in a market. A restricted tradeable quote is submitted to a market and within a certain restriction (possibly based upon price or quantity) will automatically trade against orders. Order that do not comply with restrictions are sent to the quote issuer who can choose to accept or decline the order. A counter quote is used in the negotiation model. See Volume 7 – Product: Fixed Income for example usage.

enumValues.description: Cross Trade which is executed completely or not. Both sides are treated in the same manner. This is equivalent to an All or None.

enumValues.name: CROSS_TRADE_WHICH_IS_EXECUTED_PARTIALLY_AND_THE_REST_IS_CANCELLED_ONE_SIDE_IS_FULLY_EXECUTED_THE_OTHER_SIDE_IS_PARTIALLY_EXECUTED_WITH_THE_REMAINDER_BEING_CANCELLED_THIS_IS_EQUIVALENT_TO_AN_IMMEDIATE_OR_CANCEL_ON_THE_OTHER_SIDE_NOTE_THE_CROSSPRIORITZATION
enumValues.description: Cross Trade which is executed partially and the rest is cancelled. One side is fully executed, the other side is partially executed with the remainder being cancelled. This is equivalent to an Immediate or Cancel on the other side. Note: The CrossPrioritzation (550) field may be used to indicate which side should fully execute in this scenario.

enumValues.name: CROSS_TRADE_WHICH_IS_PARTIALLY_EXECUTED_WITH_THE_UNFILLED_PORTIONS_REMAINING_ACTIVE_ONE_SIDE_OF_THE_CROSS_IS_FULLY_EXECUTED
enumValues.description: Cross trade which is partially executed with the unfilled portions remaining active. One side of the cross is fully executed (as denoted with the CrossPrioritization field), but the unfilled portion remains active.

Cross trade is executed with existing orders with the same price. In the case other orders exist with the same price, the quantity of the Cross is executed against the existing orders and quotes, the remainder of the cross is executed against the other side of the cross. The two sides potentially have different quantities.

550.name: CrossPrioritization
550.description: Indicates if one side or the other of a cross order should be prioritized. The definition of prioritization is left to the market. In some markets prioritization means which side of the cross order is applied to the market first. In other markets – prioritization may mean that the prioritized side is fully executed (sometimes referred to as the side being protected).

enumValues.name: USE_ALLOCATION_PROVIDED_WITH_THE_TRADE
enumValues.description: Use allocation provided with the trade

826.description: Identifies how the trade is to be allocated

827.name: ExpirationCycle
827.description: Part of trading cycle when an instrument expires. Field is applicable for derivatives.

829.name: TrdSubType
829.description: Further qualification to the trade type

832.name: TotNumAssignmentReports
832.description: Total Number of Assignment Reports being returned to a firm

833.name: AsgnRptID
833.description: Unique identifier for the Assignment Report

834.name: ThresholdAmount
834.description: Amount that a position has to be in the money before it is exercised.

835.name: PegMoveType
835.description: Describes whether peg is static or floats

enumValues.name: STRICT_LIMIT_IS_A_STRICT_LIMIT
enumValues.description: Strict – limit is a strict limit

enumValues.name: OR_WORSE_FOR_A_BUY_THE_PEG_LIMIT_IS_A_MINIMUM_AND_FOR_A_SELL_THE_PEG_LIMIT_IS_A_MAXIMUM
enumValues.description: Or worse – for a buy the peg limit is a minimum and for a sell the peg limit is a maximum (for use for orders which have a price range)

838.name: PegRoundDirection

enumValues.description: More aggressive – on a buy order round the price up to the nearest tick, on a sell round down to the nearest tick

enumValues.name: MORE_PASSIVE_ON_A_BUY_ORDER_ROUND_DOWN_TO_NEAREST_TICK_ON_A_SELL_ORDER_ROUND_UP_TO_NEAREST_TICK
enumValues.description: More passive – on a buy order round down to nearest tick on a sell order round up to nearest tick

838.description: If the calculated peg price is not a valid tick price, specifies whether to round the price to be more or less aggressive

839.name: PeggedPrice
839.description: The price the order is currently pegged at

841.name: DiscretionMoveType
841.description: Describes whether discretionary price is static or floats

enumValues.name: STRICT_LIMIT_IS_A_STRICT_LIMIT
enumValues.description: Strict – limit is a strict limit

844.name: DiscretionRoundDirection

enumValues.name: MORE_AGGRESSIVE_ON_A_BUY_ORDER_ROUND_THE_PRICE_UP_ROUND_UP_TO_THE_NEAREST_TICK_ON_A_SELL_ROUND_DOWN_TO_THE_NEAREST_TICK
enumValues.description: More aggressive – on a buy order round the price up to the nearest tick, on a sell round down to the nearest tick

enumValues.name: MORE_PASSIVE_ON_A_BUY_ORDER_ROUND_DOWN_TO_NEAREST_TICK_ON_A_SELL_ORDER_ROUND_UP_TO_NEAREST_TICK
enumValues.description: More passive – on a buy order round down to nearest tick on a sell order round up to nearest tick

844.description: If the calculated discretionary price is not a valid tick price, specifies whether to round the price to be more or less aggressive

845.name: DiscretionPrice
845.description: The current discretionary price of the order

847.name: TargetStrategy

enumValues.name: PARTICIPATE
enumValues.description: Participate (i.e. aim to be x percent of the market volume)

847.description: The target strategy of the order 1000+ = Reserved and available for bi-laterally agreed upon user defined values

848.name: TargetStrategyParameters
848.description: Field to allow further specification of the TargetStrategy – usage to be agreed between counterparties

849.name: ParticipationRate
849.description: For a TargetStrategy=Participate order specifies the target participation rate. For other order types this is a volume limit (i.e. do not be more than this percent of the market volume)

850.name: TargetStrategyPerformance
850.description: For communication of the performance of the order versus the target strategy

851.name: LastLiquidityInd
851.description: Indicator to identify whether this fill was a result of a liquidity provider providing or liquidity taker taking the liquidity. Applicable only for OrdStatus of Partial or Filled.

852.name: PublishTrdIndicator
852.description: Indicates if a trade should be reported via a market reporting service.

enumValues.name: QUALIFED_SERVICE_REPRESENTATIVE
enumValues.description: Qualified Service Representative (QSR) or Automatic Giveup (AGU) Contra Side Sold Short

enumValues.name: QSR_OR_AGU_CONTRA_SIDE_SOLD_SHORT_EXEMPT
enumValues.description: QSR or AGU Contra Side Sold Short Exempt

854.name: QtyType
854.description: Type of quantity specified in a quantity field

855.name: SecondaryTrdType
855.description: Additional TrdType (see tag 828) assigned to a trade by trade match system.

857.name: AllocNoOrdersType
857.description: Indicates how the orders being booked and allocated by an Allocation Instruction or Allocation Report message are identified, i.e. by explicit definition in the NoOrders group or not.

858.name: SharedCommission
858.description: Commission to be shared with a third party, e.g. as part of a directed brokerage commission sharing arrangement.

859.name: ConfirmReqID
859.description: Unique identifier for a Confirmation Request message

860.name: AvgParPx
860.description: Used to express average price as percent of par (used where AvgPx field is expressed in some other way)

861.name: ReportedPx
861.description: Reported price (used to differentiate from AvgPx on a confirmation of a marked-up or marked-down principal trade)

863.name: OrderCapacityQty
863.description: Quantity executed under a specific OrderCapacity (e.g. quantity executed as agent, quantity executed as principal)

865.name: EventType
865.description: Code to represent the type of event

867.name: EventPx
867.description: Predetermined price of issue at event, if applicable

869.name: PctAtRisk
869.description: Percent at risk due to lowest possible call.

871.name: InstrAttribType

enumValues.name: FLAT
enumValues.description: Flat (securities pay interest on a current basis but are traded without interest)

enumValues.name: INTEREST_BEARING
enumValues.description: Interest bearing (for Euro commercial paper when not issued at discount)

enumValues.name: COUPON_PERIOD
enumValues.description: Coupon period (if not semi-annual). Supply redemption date in the InstrAttribValue (872) field

enumValues.name: ESCROWED_TO_REDEMPTION_DATE_CALLABLE_SUPPLY_REDEMPTION_DATE_IN_THE_INSTRATTRIBVALUE
enumValues.description: Escrowed to redemption date – callable. Supply redemption date in the InstrAttribValue (872) field

enumValues.name: ORIGINAL_ISSUE_DISCOUNT_PRICE_SUPPLY_PRICE_IN_THE_INSTRATTRIBVALUE
enumValues.description: Original issue discount price. Supply price in the InstrAttribValue (872) field

enumValues.name: CALLABLE_WITHOUT_NOTICE_BY_MAIL_TO_HOLDER_UNLESS_REGISTERED
enumValues.description: Callable without notice by mail to holder unless registered

enumValues.name: TEXT_SUPPLY_THE_TEXT_OF_THE_ATTRIBUTE_OR_DISCLAIMER_IN_THE_INSTRATTRIBVALUE
enumValues.description: Text. Supply the text of the attribute or disclaimer in the InstrAttribValue (872) field

871.description: Code to represent the type of instrument attribute

872.name: InstrAttribValue
872.description: Attribute value appropriate to the InstrAttribType (87) field.

873.name: DatedDate
873.description: The effective date of a new securities issue determined by its underwriters. Often but not always the same as the Issue Date and the Interest Accrual Date

874.name: InterestAccrualDate
874.description: The start date used for calculating accrued interest on debt instruments which are being sold between interest payment dates. Often but not always the same as the Issue Date and the Dated Date

875.name: CPProgram
875.description: The program under which a commercial paper is issued

876.name: CPRegType
876.description: The registration type of a commercial paper issuance

877.name: UnderlyingCPProgram
877.description: The program under which the underlying commercial paper is issued

878.name: UnderlyingCPRegType
878.description: The registration type of the underlying commercial paper issuance

879.name: UnderlyingQty
879.description: Unit amount of the underlying security (par, shares, currency, etc.)

880.name: TrdMatchID
880.description: Identifier assigned to a trade by a matching system.

881.name: SecondaryTradeReportRefID
881.description: Used to refer to a previous SecondaryTradeReportRefID when amending the transaction (cancel, replace, release, or reversal).

882.name: UnderlyingDirtyPrice
882.description: Price (percent-of-par or per unit) of the underlying security or basket. "Dirty" means it includes accrued interest

883.name: UnderlyingEndPrice
883.description: Price (percent-of-par or per unit) of the underlying security or basket at the end of the agreement.

884.name: UnderlyingStartValue
884.description: Currency value attributed to this collateral at the start of the agreement

885.name: UnderlyingCurrentValue
885.description: Currency value currently attributed to this collateral

886.name: UnderlyingEndValue
886.description: Currency value attributed to this collateral at the end of the agreement

888.name: UnderlyingStipType
888.description: Type of stipulation. Same values as StipulationType (233)

889.name: UnderlyingStipValue
889.description: Value of stipulation. Same values as StipulationValue (234)

890.name: MaturityNetMoney
890.description: Net Money at maturity if Zero Coupon and maturity value is different from par value

891.name: MiscFeeBasis
891.description: Defines the unit for a miscellaneous fee.

892.name: TotNoAllocs
892.description: Total number of NoAlloc entries across all messages. Should be the sum of all NoAllocs in each message that has repeating NoAlloc entries related to the same AllocID or AllocReportID. Used to support fragmentation.

893.name: LastFragment
893.description: Indicates whether this message is the last in a sequence of messages for those messages that support fragmentation, such as Allocation Instruction, Mass Quote, Security List, Derivative Security List

897.name: NoTrades
897.description: Number of trades in repeating group.

898.name: MarginRatio
898.description: The fraction of the cash consideration that must be collateralized, expressed as a percent. A MarginRatio of 02% indicates that the value of the collateral (after deducting for "haircut") must exceed the cash consideration by 2%.

899.name: MarginExcess
899.description: Excess margin amount (deficit if value is negative)

900.name: TotalNetValue
900.description: TotalNetValue is determined as follows: At the initial collateral assignment TotalNetValue is the sum of (UnderlyingStartValue * (1-haircut)). In a collateral substitution TotalNetValue is the sum of (UnderlyingCurrentValue * (1-haircut)). For listed derivatives clearing margin management, this is the collateral value which equals (Market value * haircut)

907.name: CollAsgnRefID
907.description: Collateral Assignment Identifier to which a transaction refers

911.name: TotNumReports
911.description: Total number of reports returned in response to a request

912.name: LastRptRequested
912.description: Indicates whether this message is that last report message in response to a request, such as Order Mass Status Request.

913.name: AgreementDesc
913.description: The full name of the base standard agreement, annexes and amendments in place between the principals applicable to a financing transaction.

914.name: AgreementID
914.description: A common reference to the applicable standing agreement between the counterparties to a financing transaction.

915.name: AgreementDate
915.description: A reference to the date the underlying agreement specified by AgreementID and AgreementDesc was executed.

916.name: StartDate
916.description: Start date of a financing deal, i.e. the date the buyer pays the seller cash and takes control of the collateral

917.name: EndDate
917.description: End date of a financing deal, i.e. the date the seller reimburses the buyer and takes back control of the collateral

918.name: AgreementCurrency
918.description: Contractual currency forming the basis of a financing agreement and associated transactions. Usually, but not always, the same as the trade currency.

enumValues.name: VERSUS_PAYMENT_DELIVER
enumValues.description: "Versus. Payment": Deliver (if Sell) or Receive (if Buy) vs. (Against) Payment

enumValues.name: FREE_DELIVER
enumValues.description: "Free": Deliver (if Sell) or Receive (if Buy) Free

920.name: EndAccruedInterestAmt
920.description: Accrued Interest Amount applicable to a financing transaction on the End Date.

921.name: StartCash
921.description: Starting dirty cash consideration of a financing deal, i.e. paid to the seller on the Start Date.

922.name: EndCash
922.description: Ending dirty cash consideration of a financing deal. i.e. reimbursed to the buyer on the End Date.

923.name: UserRequestID
923.description: Unique identifier for a User Request.

924.name: UserRequestType
924.description: Indicates the action required by a User Request Message

926.name: UserStatus
926.description: Indicates the status of a user

927.name: UserStatusText
927.description: A text description associated with a user status.

928.name: StatusValue
928.description: Indicates the status of a network connection

929.name: StatusText
929.description: A text description associated with a network status.

930.name: RefCompID
930.description: Assigned value used to identify a firm.

931.name: RefSubID
931.description: Assigned value used to identify specific elements within a firm.

932.name: NetworkResponseID
932.description: Unique identifier for a network response.

933.name: NetworkRequestID
933.description: Unique identifier for a network request.

934.name: LastNetworkResponseID
934.description: Identifier of the previous Network Response message sent to a counterparty, used to allow incremental updates.

935.name: NetworkRequestType

enumValues.name: LEVEL_OF_DETAIL_THEN_NOCOMPIDS_BECOMES_REQUIRED
enumValues.description: Level of detail, then NoCompID’s becomes required

935.description: Indicates the type and level of details required for a Network Status Request Message Boolean logic applies EG If you want to subscribe for changes to certain id’s then UserRequestType =0 (8+2), Snapshot for certain ID’s = 9 (8+)

936.name: NoCompIDs
936.description: Number of CompID entries in a repeating group.

937.name: NetworkStatusResponseType
937.description: Indicates the type of Network Response Message

938.name: NoCollInquiryQualifier
938.description: Number of CollInquiryQualifier entries in a repeating group.

940.name: AffirmStatus
940.description: Identifies the status of the ConfirmationAck

941.name: UnderlyingStrikeCurrency
941.description: Currency in which the strike price of an underlying instrument is denominated

942.name: LegStrikeCurrency
942.description: Currency in which the strike price of an instrument leg of a multileg instrument is denominated

943.name: TimeBracket
943.description: A code that represents a time interval in which a fill or trade occurred. Required for US futures markets.

944.name: CollAction
944.description: Action proposed for an Underlying Instrument instance

946.name: CollInquiryResult

enumValues.name: NO_COLLATERAL_FOUND_FOR_THE_TRADE_SPECIFIED
enumValues.description: No collateral found for the trade specified

enumValues.name: NO_COLLATERAL_FOUND_FOR_THE_ORDER_SPECIFIED
enumValues.description: No collateral found for the order specified

946.description: Result returned in response to Collateral Inquiry 4000+ Reserved and available for bi-laterally agreed upon user-defined values

947.name: StrikeCurrency
947.description: Currency in which the StrikePrice is denominated.

948.name: NoNested3PartyIDs
948.description: Number of Nested3PartyID (949), Nested3PartyIDSource (950), and Nested3PartyRole (95) entries

949.name: Nested3PartyID
949.description: PartyID value within a "third instance" Nested repeating group. Same values as PartyID (448)

950.name: Nested3PartyIDSource
950.description: PartyIDSource value within a "third instance" Nested repeating group. Same values as PartyIDSource (447)

951.name: Nested3PartyRole
951.description: PartyRole value within a "third instance" Nested repeating group. Same values as PartyRole (452)

953.name: Nested3PartySubID
953.description: PartySubID value within a "third instance" Nested repeating group. Same values as PartySubID (523)

954.name: Nested3PartySubIDType
954.description: PartySubIDType value within a "third instance" Nested repeating group. Same values as PartySubIDType (803)

955.name: LegContractSettlMonth
955.description: Specifies when the contract (i.e. MBS/TBA) will settle.

956.name: LegInterestAccrualDate
956.description: The start date used for calculating accrued interest on debt instruments which are being sold between interest payment dates. Often but not always the same as the Issue Date and the Dated Date
